content.text:
It's well-defined in RBS0 — "Enclave Network" is a formal quoin (`at_enclave_network`): the isolated network connecting a bottle to its sentry, the only communication path for the bottle.

Now let me check whether README.md already has it.

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
Not anchored in README yet. My read:

**Yes, this earns an anchor.** "Enclave" is a load-bearing concept — it's the network isolation layer that makes crucible security work. A learner reading the First Crucible handbook encounters sentry, pentacle, bottle, and the network that connects them. Sentry/pentacle/bottle all have anchors already. The enclave is the fourth structural component — the network itself — and it's the one that carries the security semantics (iptables + dnsmasq enforcement happens *on* the enclave).

The paddock's teaching voice principle ("nouns introduced in relation to each other") fits naturally: *"The Enclave is the isolated network connecting the Bottle to its Sentry — the Bottle's only path to the outside world."*

It would slot into the README alongside the existing Crucible/Sentry/Pentacle/Bottle cluster, and into `rbyc_common.sh` as `RBYC_ENCLAVE`. Then handbook prose can use `${RBYC_ENCLAVE}` instead of the bare word.

Want me to draft the README anchor and the RBYC constant?

message.content: yes, then incorporate into this onboarding handbook.

content.content: 206:### Crucible Lifecycle
No matches found

1:# Recipe Bottle 3:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) provides two independent container image capabilities: 18:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) is a set of bash scripts enabling enterprise grade container image management intended for incorporation into any project. 22:[Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) goal is a workflow where every container image has a verified origin and a controlled version, running behind appropriate network safeguards. 27: 32:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) is organized around two independent capabilities: the [Foundry](#Foundry) builds container images with verifiable [provenance](#Provenance), and the [Crucible](#Crucible) runs untrusted images with enforced network isolation. 35:All configuration flows through [Regimes](#Regime) — structured `.env` files with typed validation, each with its own render and validate commands. 39:Every operation is launched through a [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) — a shell script in the `tt/` directory. 41:Type `tt/rbw-` and the shell narrows to all [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) operations; type `tt/rbw-h` to see just the [Hallmark](#Hallmark) commands. 44:Every state-changing [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) writes three [Log](#Log) files to the directory named by `BURS_LOG_DIR` in your [BURS](#BURS) station file: a stable-name file (always the same path — easy for tooling to locate and evaluate the most recent run), a per-command file (same command name across runs — tools like SlickEdit sense diffs between executions), and a timestamped historical file (permanent record). 48:A [Transcript](#Transcript) is a single file capturing key decision points and state transitions within a [Tabtarget's](#Tabtarget) execution. 51:The [Output](#Output) directory is a fixed-path staging area cleared and recreated before each [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) runs. 61:## Foundry 63:[Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) remote build orchestration system for producing, attesting, and distributing container images via Google Cloud Build and Google Artifact Registry. 71:### Depot 79:- **[Tethered](#Tethered)** — Build egress mode allowing public internet access during Cloud Build. [Tethered](#Tethered) builds pull base images from upstream registries at build time — simpler to set up, but dependent on upstream availability. 80:- **[Airgap](#Airgap)** — Build egress mode with no public internet access during Cloud Build. 85:### Manor 91:### Payor 97:### Governor 103:### Director 109:### Retriever 115:### Vessel 121:### Hallmark 128:### Foundry Lifecycle 130:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) uses a role-based security model with four roles, each building on the previous: 142:#### Establishment and Provisioning 152:#### Credential Distribution 157:#### Build and Retrieve 165:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) builds container images on Google Cloud Build (GCB) and stores them in Google Artifact Registry (GAR): 177:## Crucible 179:The distinctive case [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) addresses is *running untrusted code*: third-party tooling, experimental packages, binaries with uncertain [provenance](#Provenance). 181:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) assembles a [Crucible](#Crucible) — three cooperating containers where a [Sentry](#Sentry) enforces network policy — without requiring modifications to existing container images. 182:The [Bottle](#Bottle) container runs unmodified, in a network namespace prepared by a privileged [Pentacle](#Pentacle), with all egress flowing through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway. 184:The [Sentry](#Sentry)/[Pentacle](#Pentacle)/[Bottle](#Bottle) triad running together as one unit for a [Nameplate](#Nameplate). 188:### Nameplate 190:Per-[Vessel](#Vessel) configuration tying a [Sentry](#Sentry) and [Bottle](#Bottle) together into a runnable [Crucible](#Crucible). 192:Each [Nameplate](#Nameplate) declares its [Vessel](#Vessel) selections, [Hallmark](#Hallmark) pins, and the network policy that the [Sentry](#Sentry) enforces. 194:### Containers 196:- **[Sentry](#Sentry)** — Security container enforcing network policies via `iptables` and `dnsmasq`. 197:The [Sentry](#Sentry) applies two layers of egress policy: DNS-level filtering (only allowed domain names resolve) and IP-level filtering (only allowed CIDR ranges pass). 198:A compromised [Bottle](#Bottle) cannot bypass either layer — the [Sentry](#Sentry) is the sole gateway between the [Bottle](#Bottle) and the outside network. 199:- **[Pentacle](#Pentacle)** — Privileged container establishing the network namespace shared with the [Bottle](#Bottle). 200:The [Pentacle](#Pentacle) runs briefly with elevated privileges to create the network topology, then remains as the namespace anchor. 201:Security policies are enforced from the first packet because the [Sentry](#Sentry) configures the namespace before the [Bottle](#Bottle) starts. 202:- **[Bottle](#Bottle)** — Your workload container, running unmodified in a controlled network environment. 203:The [Bottle](#Bottle) has no direct network access — all traffic routes through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway in a namespace prepared by the [Pentacle](#Pentacle). 204:Any existing container image can run as a [Bottle](#Bottle) without modification. 206:### Crucible Lifecycle 208:[Charge](#Charge) the [Crucible](#Crucible) for a [Nameplate](#Nameplate) to start the [Sentry](#Sentry), [Pentacle](#Pentacle), and [Bottle](#Bottle) together — the [Bottle](#Bottle) is ready for interactive use immediately. 209:[Rack](#Rack) the [Bottle](#Bottle) to shell in, [Hail](#Hail) the [Sentry](#Sentry) to inspect the gateway, or [Scry](#Scry) the network to observe traffic across [Crucible](#Crucible) containers. 213:### Reference Nameplates 216:Each pairs a [Sentry](#Sentry) with a [Bottle](#Bottle) [Vessel](#Vessel) and defines the network policy for that deployment target. 218:**[ccyolo](#ccyolo)** — Claude Code sandbox for network-contained AI development. 219:The [ccyolo](#ccyolo) [Crucible](#Crucible) runs Claude Code inside a [Bottle](#Bottle) that can only reach Anthropic — SSH entry from the workstation, OAuth authentication via copy/paste, everything else blocked. 223:**[tadmor](#tadmor)** — Adversarial security testing [Crucible](#Crucible) for daily iteration. 224:The [tadmor](#tadmor) [Nameplate](#Nameplate) pairs the [Sentry](#Sentry) with the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack [Vessel](#Vessel) under a restrictive network allowlist, consuming [Kludged](#Kludge) [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) for fast author-test-iterate cycles. 225:The [Theurge](#Theurge) test orchestrator [Charges](#Charge) [tadmor](#tadmor) and dispatches curated escape attempts to validate that the [Sentry's](#Sentry) containment holds under adversarial conditions. 227:**[moriah](#moriah)** — Adversarial security testing [Crucible](#Crucible) for the airgap supply chain. 228:The [moriah](#moriah) [Nameplate](#Nameplate) pairs the [Sentry](#Sentry) with the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack [Vessel](#Vessel) under the same restrictive network allowlist as [tadmor](#tadmor), consuming [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) [Ordained](#Ordain) end-to-end on the [Airgap](#Airgap) pool. 235:### Infrastructure 237:**[Establish](#Establish)** — Guided setup of a new [Manor](#Manor) — creates the [Manor's](#Manor) GCP project and configures the OAuth consent screen through the Google Cloud Console. 240:**[Install](#Install)** — Ingest OAuth client credentials from a downloaded JSON key file. 244:**[Levy](#Levy)** — Provision a new [Depot's](#Depot) GCP infrastructure. 248:**[Unmake](#Unmake)** — Permanently destroy a [Depot's](#Depot) GCP infrastructure — project, artifact registry, storage bucket, and all contents. 252:**[Refresh](#Refresh)** — Refresh an expired [Payor](#Payor) OAuth token. 256:**[Quota](#Quota)** — Review Cloud Build capacity and usage. 259:### Credentials 261:**[Mantle](#Mantle)** — Create or replace the [Governor](#Governor) service account for a [Depot](#Depot). 264:**[Knight](#Knight)** — Create a [Director](#Director) service account. 267:**[Charter](#Charter)** — Create a [Retriever](#Retriever) service account. 270:**[Forfeit](#Forfeit)** — Revoke a service account credential. 274:**[List Service Accounts](#ListSAs)** — Inventory all service accounts issued within a [Depot](#Depot). 277:### Supply Chain 279:**[Enshrine](#Enshrine)** — Mirror upstream base images into your [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 282:**[Reliquary](#Reliquary)** — Co-versioned set of builder tool images (skopeo, docker, gcloud, syft) inscribed from upstream into the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 286:### Building 288:**[Ordain](#Ordain)** — Create a [Hallmark](#Hallmark) with full attestation — the production build operation. 292:**[Conjure](#Conjure)** — Cloud Build creates the image from source. 296:**[Bind](#Bind)** — Mirror an upstream image pinned by digest. 300:**[Graft](#Graft)** — Push a locally-built image to the [Depot](#Depot) registry. 304:**[Kludge](#Kludge)** — Build a [Vessel](#Vessel) image locally for fast iteration, without [Depot](#Depot) registry push. 308:**[Pouch](#Pouch)** — Build context packaged as a FROM SCRATCH OCI image and pushed to the [Depot's](#Depot) registry before a Cloud Build job runs. 312:### Verification 314:**[Tally](#Tally)** — Inventory [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) in the [Depot](#Depot) registry by health status. 318:**[Rekon](#Rekon)** — Raw listing of image tags in the [Depot](#Depot) registry for a [Vessel](#Vessel) package. 322:**[Vouch](#Vouch)** — Cryptographic attestation proving a [Hallmark](#Hallmark) was built by trusted infrastructure. 326:**[About](#About)** — Build metadata and [software bill of materials](#SBOM) for a [Hallmark](#Hallmark). 330:**[Plumb](#Plumb)** — Inspect an artifact's [provenance](#Provenance) — [SBOM](#SBOM), build info, and [Vouch](#Vouch) chain. 334:### Distribution 336:**[Summon](#Summon)** — Pull a [Hallmark](#Hallmark) image from the [Depot](#Depot) to your local machine. 339:**[Wrest](#Wrest)** — Pull a specific image from the [Depot](#Depot) registry by reference. 343:### Removal 345:**[Abjure](#Abjure)** — Remove a [Hallmark's](#Hallmark) artifacts from the [Depot's](#Depot) registry — the `-image`, [`-about`](#About), and [`-vouch`](#Vouch) tags deleted as a coherent unit. 349:**[Jettison](#Jettison)** — Delete a specific image tag from the [Depot's](#Depot) registry. 353:### Diagnostics 355:**[List Depots](#ListDepots)** — Inventory all active [Depots](#Depot) visible to the current [Payor](#Payor) credentials. 358:**[JWT Probe](#JWTProbe)** — Test service account authentication. 361:**[OAuth Probe](#OAuthProbe)** — Test [Payor](#Payor) OAuth authentication. 368:### Lifecycle 370:**[Charge](#Charge)** — Start the [Sentry](#Sentry)/[Pentacle](#Pentacle)/[Bottle](#Bottle) triad for a [Nameplate](#Nameplate). 371:[Charging](#Charge) brings up the [Crucible](#Crucible) in dependency order: [Pentacle](#Pentacle) creates the namespace, [Sentry](#Sentry) configures policy, then the [Bottle](#Bottle) starts with its network already constrained. 373:**[Quench](#Quench)** — Stop and clean up a [Charged](#Charge) [Nameplate's](#Nameplate) containers. 376:### Interaction 378:**[Rack](#Rack)** — Shell into a [Bottle](#Bottle) container. 379:[Racking](#Rack) opens an interactive session inside the running workload — for debugging, inspecting state, or running commands as the [Bottle](#Bottle) user would experience them. 381:**[Hail](#Hail)** — Shell into a [Sentry](#Sentry) container. 384:**[Scry](#Scry)** — Observe network traffic across [Crucible](#Crucible) containers. 385:[Scrying](#Scry) captures packets on the [Crucible's](#Crucible) network interfaces — for verifying that blocked traffic is actually blocked, diagnosing connectivity issues, or watching the [Sentry's](#Sentry) filtering in action. 391:- **[Ifrit](#Ifrit)** — Adversarial attack [Vessel](#Vessel) purpose-built to run inside a [Bottle](#Bottle), seeking escape. 392:The [Ifrit](#Ifrit) carries scapy (arbitrary packet construction), strace (syscall boundary probing), and a minimal footprint — tools chosen to probe every surface the [Sentry's](#Sentry) containment exposes. 394:- **[Theurge](#Theurge)** — Test orchestrator running on the host, outside the [Crucible](#Crucible). 395:The [Theurge](#Theurge) [Charges](#Charge) a [Crucible](#Crucible) with the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) as its [Bottle](#Bottle), then dispatches curated, reproducible, version-controlled attack scripts targeting specific surfaces: DNS exfiltration, ICMP covert channels, cloud metadata probing, namespace breakout, and direct IP bypass attempts. 396:Each attack runs inside the [Bottle](#Bottle) while the [Theurge](#Theurge) simultaneously observes the [Sentry's](#Sentry) network from outside — confirming that blocked traffic is actually blocked, not merely unrequested. 398:The escape tests were developed through adversarial Claude Code sessions with full visibility into the [Sentry's](#Sentry) source, configuration, and the [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) specification. 403:## Appendix: Supply Chain Provenance 407:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) achieves [SLSA](https://slsa.dev) v1.0 Build Level 3 for [Conjure](#Conjure) builds, auto-generated by Google Cloud Build. 421:## Appendix: Software Bill of Materials 426:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) generates an [SBOM](#SBOM) for every build using [Syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft), scanning each per-platform image during the [About](#About) assembly step. 437:## Appendix: Build Isolation 439:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) supports two build egress profiles — [Tethered](#Tethered) and [Airgap](#Airgap) — that determine whether a Cloud Build job can reach the public internet. 464:- **Crucible conduit for cloud services** - Encrypted tunnel from the [Sentry](#Sentry) to a VPC hosting PrivateLink endpoints, enabling [Bottles](#Bottle) to reach cloud AI services (AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI) without exposing floating cloud IP ranges in the CIDR allowlist. 465:WireGuard terminated at the [Sentry](#Sentry) replaces per-service IP tracking with a single stable VPC CIDR. 466:Near-term, allowlist-only [Nameplates](#Nameplate) targeting specific service CIDRs and domains work today with existing [Sentry](#Sentry) machinery. 474:[Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) Cloud Build architecture uses private pools, which are the prerequisite for VPC enforcement; enabling the controls themselves is deferred until organizational policy or external distribution requires them. 475:If a VPC is stood up for the crucible conduit architecture, the VPC-SC perimeter should serve both Cloud Build [egress lockdown](#BuildIsolation) and [Bottle](#Bottle) conduit consumers. 480:- **CDN-aware IP gating** - When allowed domains are CDN-hosted (e.g. Cloudflare), the [Sentry's](#Sentry) CIDR allowlist becomes coarse: DNS-level gating remains precise, but IP-level gating is porous across shared CDN address ranges. 487:- **[Crucible](#Crucible)-to-[Crucible](#Crucible) networking** - Under the current [Sentry](#Sentry) model, [Bottles](#Bottle) have no direct network path to each other; any inter-[Bottle](#Bottle) communication would route through their respective [Sentries](#Sentry). 492:This repository is the reference implementation of [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle). 502:| `│ └── rbk/` | Recipe Bottle Kit — domain logic | 508:| `│ │ └── rbrn.env` | [RBRN](#RBRN) — [Sentry](#Sentry) + Claude Code, Anthropic-only allowlist | 510:| `│ │ └── rbrn.env` | [RBRN](#RBRN) — [Sentry](#Sentry) + [Ifrit](#Ifrit), restrictive allowlist | 512:| `│ │ └── rbrn.env` | [RBRN](#RBRN) — [Sentry](#Sentry) + [Ifrit](#Ifrit), restrictive allowlist | 514:| `│ │ └── rbrn.env` | [RBRN](#RBRN) — [Sentry](#Sentry) + Jupyter, academic-domain allowlist | 516:| `│ └── rbrn.env` | [RBRN](#RBRN) — [Sentry](#Sentry) + PlantUML, no-egress allowlist | 518:| ` ├── common-sentry-context/` | Shared [Sentry](#Sentry)/[Pentacle](#Pentacle) build context | 520:| ` │ ├── rbjs_sentry.sh` | [Sentry](#Sentry) runtime — policy engine | 521:| ` │ └── rbjp_pentacle.sh` | [Pentacle](#Pentacle) runtime — namespace setup | 522:| ` ├── rbev-sentry-deb-tether/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) — [Sentry](#Sentry) (tethered, upstream pull) | 524:| ` ├── rbev-sentry-deb-airgap/` | [Conjure](#Conjure) — [Sentry](#Sentry) (airgapped, enshrined bases) | 551:**[BURC](#BURC)** — Project structure configuration, in the repo. 554:**[BURS](#BURS)** — Developer workstation configuration. 558:**[RBRR](#RBRR)** — [Depot](#Depot) identity and build configuration — populated during [Levy](#Levy), consumed by [Director](#Director) and [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. 560:**[RBRP](#RBRP)** — [Manor](#Manor) identity — billing account, OAuth client ID, operator email, and the [Manor's](#Manor) GCP project. 563:**[RBRO](#RBRO)** — [Payor](#Payor) OAuth credentials — client secret and refresh token. 566:**[RBRA](#RBRA)** — Role credentials resident on user workstation, enabling [Governor](#Governor), [Director](#Director), or [Retriever](#Retriever) operations. 569:**[RBRV](#RBRV)** — [Vessel](#Vessel) configuration specifying [Bind](#Bind), [Conjure](#Conjure), or [Graft](#Graft) mode for creating [Hallmarks](#Hallmark). 571:**[RBRN](#RBRN)** — Per-[Nameplate](#Nameplate) [Crucible](#Crucible) configuration mapping two [Vessels](#Vessel) — [Sentry](#Sentry) and [Bottle](#Bottle) — with runtime and [Hallmark](#Hallmark) assignments.

toolUseResult.content: 1:# Recipe Bottle 3:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) provides two independent container image capabilities: 18:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) is a set of bash scripts enabling enterprise grade container image management intended for incorporation into any project. 22:[Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) goal is a workflow where every container image has a verified origin and a controlled version, running behind appropriate network safeguards. 27: 32:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) is organized around two independent capabilities: the [Foundry](#Foundry) builds container images with verifiable [provenance](#Provenance), and the [Crucible](#Crucible) runs untrusted images with enforced network isolation. 35:All configuration flows through [Regimes](#Regime) — structured `.env` files with typed validation, each with its own render and validate commands. 39:Every operation is launched through a [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) — a shell script in the `tt/` directory. 41:Type `tt/rbw-` and the shell narrows to all [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) operations; type `tt/rbw-h` to see just the [Hallmark](#Hallmark) commands. 44:Every state-changing [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) writes three [Log](#Log) files to the directory named by `BURS_LOG_DIR` in your [BURS](#BURS) station file: a stable-name file (always the same path — easy for tooling to locate and evaluate the most recent run), a per-command file (same command name across runs — tools like SlickEdit sense diffs between executions), and a timestamped historical file (permanent record). 48:A [Transcript](#Transcript) is a single file capturing key decision points and state transitions within a [Tabtarget's](#Tabtarget) execution. 51:The [Output](#Output) directory is a fixed-path staging area cleared and recreated before each [Tabtarget](#Tabtarget) runs. 61:## Foundry 63:[Recipe Bottle's](#RecipeBottle) remote build orchestration system for producing, attesting, and distributing container images via Google Cloud Build and Google Artifact Registry. 71:### Depot 79:- **[Tethered](#Tethered)** — Build egress mode allowing public internet access during Cloud Build. [Tethered](#Tethered) builds pull base images from upstream registries at build time — simpler to set up, but dependent on upstream availability. 80:- **[Airgap](#Airgap)** — Build egress mode with no public internet access during Cloud Build. 85:### Manor 91:### Payor 97:### Governor 103:### Director 109:### Retriever 115:### Vessel 121:### Hallmark 128:### Foundry Lifecycle 130:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) uses a role-based security model with four roles, each building on the previous: 142:#### Establishment and Provisioning 152:#### Credential Distribution 157:#### Build and Retrieve 165:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) builds container images on Google Cloud Build (GCB) and stores them in Google Artifact Registry (GAR): 177:## Crucible 179:The distinctive case [Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) addresses is *running untrusted code*: third-party tooling, experimental packages, binaries with uncertain [provenance](#Provenance). 181:[Recipe Bottle](#RecipeBottle) assembles a [Crucible](#Crucible) — three cooperating containers where a [Sentry](#Sentry) enforces network policy — without requiring modifications to existing container images. 182:The [Bottle](#Bottle) container runs unmodified, in a network namespace prepared by a privileged [Pentacle](#Pentacle), with all egress flowing through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway. 184:The [Sentry](#Sentry)/[Pentacle](#Pentacle)/[Bottle](#Bottle) triad running together as one unit for a [Nameplate](#Nameplate). 188:### Nameplate 190:Per-[Vessel](#Vessel) configuration tying a [Sentry](#Sentry) and [Bottle](#Bottle) together into a runnable [Crucible](#Crucible). 192:Each [Nameplate](#Nameplate) declares its [Vessel](#Vessel) selections, [Hallmark](#Hallmark) pins, and the network policy that the [Sentry](#Sentry) enforces. 194:### Containers 196:- **[Sentry](#Sentry)** — Security container enforcing network policies via `iptables` and `dnsmasq`. 197:The [Sentry](#Sentry) applies two layers of egress policy: DNS-level filtering (only allowed domain names resolve) and IP-level filtering (only allowed CIDR ranges pass). 198:A compromised [Bottle](#Bottle) cannot bypass either layer — the [Sentry](#Sentry) is the sole gateway between the [Bottle](#Bottle) and the outside network. 199:- **[Pentacle](#Pentacle)** — Privileged container establishing the network namespace shared with the [Bottle](#Bottle). 200:The [Pentacle](#Pentacle) runs briefly with elevated privileges to create the network topology, then remains as the namespace anchor. 201:Security policies are enforced from the first packet because the [Sentry](#Sentry) configures the namespace before the [Bottle](#Bottle) starts. 202:- **[Bottle](#Bottle)** — Your workload container, running unmodified in a controlled network environment. 203:The [Bottle](#Bottle) has no direct network access — all traffic routes through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway in a namespace prepared by the [Pentacle](#Pentacle). 204:Any existing container image can run as a [Bottle](#Bottle) without modification. 206:### Crucible Lifecycle 208:[Charge](#Charge) the [Crucible](#Crucible) for a [Nameplate](#Nameplate) to start the [Sentry](#Sentry), [Pentacle](#Pentacle), and [Bottle](#Bottle) together — the [Bottle](#Bottle) is ready for interactive use immediately. 209:[Rack](#Rack) the [Bottle](#Bottle) to shell in, [Hail](#Hail) the [Sentry](#Sentry) to inspect the gateway, or [Scry](#Scry) the network to observe traffic across [Crucible](#Crucible) containers. 213:### Reference Nameplates 216:Each pairs a [Sentry](#Sentry) with a [Bottle](#Bottle) [Vessel](#Vessel) and defines the network policy for that deployment target. 218:**[ccyolo](#ccyolo)** — Claude Code sandbox for network-contained AI development. 219:The [ccyolo](#ccyolo) [Crucible](#Crucible) runs Claude Code inside a [Bottle](#Bottle) that can only reach Anthropic — SSH entry from the workstation, OAuth authentication via copy/paste, everything else blocked. 223:**[tadmor](#tadmor)** — Adversarial security testing [Crucible](#Crucible) for daily iteration. 224:The [tadmor](#tadmor) [Nameplate](#Nameplate) pairs the [Sentry](#Sentry) with the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack [Vessel](#Vessel) under a restrictive network allowlist, consuming [Kludged](#Kludge) [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) for fast author-test-iterate cycles. 225:The [Theurge](#Theurge) test orchestrator [Charges](#Charge) [tadmor](#tadmor) and dispatches curated escape attempts to validate that the [Sentry's](#Sentry) containment holds under adversarial conditions. 227:**[moriah](#moriah)** — Adversarial security testing [Crucible](#Crucible) for the airgap supply chain. 228:The [moriah](#moriah) [Nameplate](#Nameplate) pairs the [Sentry](#Sentry) with the [Ifrit](#Ifrit) attack [Vessel](#Vessel) under the same restrictive network allowlist as [tadmor](#tadmor), consuming [Hallmarks](#Hallmark) [Ordained](#Ordain) end-to-end on the [Airgap](#Airgap) pool. 235:### Infrastructure 237:**[Establish](#Establish)** — Guided setup of a new [Manor](#Manor) — creates the [Manor's](#Manor) GCP project and configures the OAuth consent screen through the Google Cloud Console. 240:**[Install](#Install)** — Ingest OAuth client credentials from a downloaded JSON key file. storage bucket, and all contents. 252:**[Refresh](#Refresh)** — Refresh an expired [Payor](#Payor) OAuth token. 256:**[Quota](#Quota)** — Review Cloud Build capacity and usage. 259:### Credentials content.content: 194 ### Containers 195 196 - **[Sentry](#Sentry)** — Security container enforcing network policies via `iptables` and `dnsmasq`. 197 The [Sentry](#Sentry) applies two layers of egress policy: DNS-level filtering (only allowed domain names resolve) and IP-level filtering (only allowed CIDR ranges pass). 198 A compromised [Bottle](#Bottle) cannot bypass either layer — the [Sentry](#Sentry) is the sole gateway between the [Bottle](#Bottle) and the outside network. 199 - **[Pentacle](#Pentacle)** — Privileged container establishing the network namespace shared with the [Bottle](#Bottle). 200 The [Pentacle](#Pentacle) runs briefly with elevated privileges to create the network topology, then remains as the namespace anchor. 201 Security policies are enforced from the first packet because the [Sentry](#Sentry) configures the namespace before the [Bottle](#Bottle) starts. 202 - **[Bottle](#Bottle)** — Your workload container, running unmodified in a controlled network environment. 203 The [Bottle](#Bottle) has no direct network access — all traffic routes through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway in a namespace prepared by the [Pentacle](#Pentacle). 204 Any existing container image can run as a [Bottle](#Bottle) without modification. 205 206 ### Crucible Lifecycle 207

file.content: ### Containers - **[Sentry](#Sentry)** — Security container enforcing network policies via `iptables` and `dnsmasq`. The [Sentry](#Sentry) applies two layers of egress policy: DNS-level filtering (only allowed domain names resolve) and IP-level filtering (only allowed CIDR ranges pass). A compromised [Bottle](#Bottle) cannot bypass either layer — the [Sentry](#Sentry) is the sole gateway between the [Bottle](#Bottle) and the outside network. - **[Pentacle](#Pentacle)** — Privileged container establishing the network namespace shared with the [Bottle](#Bottle). The [Pentacle](#Pentacle) runs briefly with elevated privileges to create the network topology, then remains as the namespace anchor. Security policies are enforced from the first packet because the [Sentry](#Sentry) configures the namespace before the [Bottle](#Bottle) starts. - **[Bottle](#Bottle)** — Your workload container, running unmodified in a controlled network environment. The [Bottle](#Bottle) has no direct network access — all traffic routes through the [Sentry](#Sentry) gateway in a namespace prepared by the [Pentacle](#Pentacle). Any existing container image can run as a [Bottle](#Bottle) without modification. ### Crucible Lifecycle

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Vocabulary — linked term constants
#
# Kindled after regime loads (needs RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL).  Does not
# source or kindle anything itself — the consuming _cli.sh is responsible
# for sourcing buym_yelp.sh and this file, then calling zrbyc_kindle.
#
# Each constant is a diastema-marked yelp fragment suitable for
# interpolation into buh_line calls:
#   buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
#
# Variant forms (_S plural, _P possessive) link to the base anchor
# with alternate display text.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBYC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBYC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Module kindle

zrbyc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc already kindled"

  zbuym_sentinel

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # Helper: yawp then capture into named readonly
  # Usage: zrbyc_yk RBYC_TERM base_url anchor [display]
  zrbyc_yk() {
    buyy_link_yawp "${2}" "${3}" "${4:-}"
    readonly "${1}=${z_buym_yelp}"
  }

  # --- Paddock anchor inventory (38 base terms) ---

  # Operations
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ORDAIN    "${z_docs}" "Ordain"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CONJURE   "${z_docs}" "Conjure"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BIND      "${z_docs}" "Bind"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_GRAFT     "${z_docs}" "Graft"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_KLUDGE    "${z_docs}" "Kludge"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ENSHRINE  "${z_docs}" "Enshrine"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SUMMON    "${z_docs}" "Summon"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PLUMB     "${z_docs}" "Plumb"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TALLY     "${z_docs}" "Tally"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VOUCH     "${z_docs}" "Vouch"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LEVY      "${z_docs}" "Levy"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CHARGE    "${z_docs}" "Charge"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_QUENCH    "${z_docs}" "Quench"

  # Artifacts and infrastructure
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VESSEL    "${z_docs}" "Vessel"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_HALLMARK  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DEPOT     "${z_docs}" "Depot"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RELIQUARY "${z_docs}" "Reliquary"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_POUCH     "${z_docs}" "Pouch"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_NAMEPLATE "${z_docs}" "Nameplate"

  # Egress modes
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TETHERED  "${z_docs}" "Tethered"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_AIRGAP    "${z_docs}" "Airgap"

  # Crucible components
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CRUCIBLE  "${z_docs}" "Crucible"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY    "${z_docs}" "Sentry"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY_S  "${z_docs}" "Sentry" "Sentries"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY_P  "${z_docs}" "Sentry" "Sentry's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PENTACLE  "${z_docs}" "Pentacle"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BOTTLE    "${z_docs}" "Bottle"

  # Roles
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PAYOR     "${z_docs}" "Payor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_GOVERNOR  "${z_docs}" "Governor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DIRECTOR  "${z_docs}" "Director"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RETRIEVER "${z_docs}" "Retriever"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CHARTER   "${z_docs}" "Charter"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_KNIGHT    "${z_docs}" "Knight"

  # Infrastructure concepts
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TABTARGET "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REGIME    "${z_docs}" "Regime"

  # Regime file types
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRP      "${z_docs}" "RBRP"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRR      "${z_docs}" "RBRR"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRN      "${z_docs}" "RBRN"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRV      "${z_docs}" "RBRV"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BURC      "${z_docs}" "BURC"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BURS      "${z_docs}" "BURS"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRO      "${z_docs}" "RBRO"

  # --- Additional anchors used in handbooks ---

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE "${z_docs}" "" "Recipe Bottle"

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RACK      "${z_docs}" "Rack"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_MANOR     "${z_docs}" "Manor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRA      "${z_docs}" "RBRA"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CCYOLO    "${z_docs}" "ccyolo"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ABJURE    "${z_docs}" "Abjure"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REKON     "${z_docs}" "Rekon"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LOG       "${z_docs}" "Log"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TRANSCRIPT "${z_docs}" "Transcript"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_OUTPUT    "${z_docs}" "Output"

  # --- Variant forms (plural, possessive) ---

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_HALLMARKS  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark"   "Hallmarks"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VESSELS    "${z_docs}" "Vessel"     "Vessels"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DIRECTORS  "${z_docs}" "Director"   "Directors"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RETRIEVERS "${z_docs}" "Retriever"  "Retrievers"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TABTARGETS "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"  "Tabtargets"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REGIMES    "${z_docs}" "Regime"     "Regimes"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LOGS       "${z_docs}" "Log"        "Logs"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_MANORS     "${z_docs}" "Manor"      "Manor's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PAYORS     "${z_docs}" "Payor"      "Payor's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VOUCHED    "${z_docs}" "Vouch"      "Vouched"

  # --- Probe markers (pass/warn for handbook status probes) ---

  buyy_pass_yawp " [*] "; readonly RBYC_PROBE_YES="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_warn_yawp " [ ] "; readonly RBYC_PROBE_NO="${z_buym_yelp}"

  unset -f zrbyc_yk
  readonly ZRBYC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbyc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbyc not kindled - call zrbyc_kindle first"
}

# eof

26	######################################################################
27	# First Crucible — local builds onboarding

29	# Frame 4-refined handbook: teaching prose + probes + tabtarget refs.
30	# Target learner: crucible explorer — local-only, zero cloud, fast
31	# iteration for security exploration. Zero registry, zero SA credentials.
32	#
33	# Nameplate: ccyolo  (Claude Code sandbox, Anthropic-only network)
34	# Vessels:   rbev-sentry-deb-tether (sentry), rbev-bottle-ccyolo (bottle)
35	#
36	# ₢A6AAC DRAFT — content ready for implementation review.
37	# Infrastructure gaps are marked with [INFRA-NEEDED] comments.
38	# Assumptions are marked with [ASSUMPTION].
39	
40	rbho_first_crucible() {
41	  zrbho_sentinel
42	
43	  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
44	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
45	
46	  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
47	  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
48	  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
49	  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
50	  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
51	  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"
52	
53	  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
54	  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-fk";                 local -r z_cmd_rbw_fk="${z_buym_yelp}"
55	  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-cKB";                local -r z_cmd_rbw_cKB="${z_buym_yelp}"
56	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=";  local -r z_cmd_sentry_hallmark="${z_buym_yelp}"
57	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}";     local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"
58	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_sentry_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
59	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_bottle_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
60	  buyy_cmd_yawp "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"; local -r z_example_tag="${z_buym_yelp}"
61	
62	  # --- Probes ---
63	
64	  # Docker runtime
65	  local z_has_docker=0
66	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1
67	
68	  # Nameplate exists
69	  local z_nameplate_exists=0
70	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1
71	
72	  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
73	  local z_sentry_hallmark=""
74	  local z_bottle_hallmark=""
75	  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0
76	  local z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
77	  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
78	    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
79	    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
80	    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
81	    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
82	  fi
83	
84	  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
85	  local z_sentry_image_exists=0
86	  local z_bottle_image_exists=0
87	  local z_line=""
88	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
89	    local -r z_fc_images_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}4_repotag.txt"
90	    local -r z_fc_images_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
91	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
92	         > "${z_fc_images_out}" 2>"${z_fc_images_err}"; then
93	      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
94	        case "${z_line}" in
95	          *"${z_sentry_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_sentry_image_exists=1 ;;
96	          *"${z_bottle_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_bottle_image_exists=1 ;;
97	        esac
98	      done < "${z_fc_images_out}"
99	    fi
100	  fi
101	
102	  # Crucible charged
103	  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
104	  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
105	  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
106	  local z_crucible_charged=0
107	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
108	    local -r z_fc_ps_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_PS_PREFIX}2_names.txt"
109	    local -r z_fc_ps_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}6_ps.txt"
110	    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" > "${z_fc_ps_out}" 2>"${z_fc_ps_err}"; then
111	      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
112	        case "${z_line}" in "${z_moniker}-bottle") z_crucible_charged=1; break ;; esac
113	      done < "${z_fc_ps_out}"
114	    fi
115	  fi
116	
117	  # --- Header ---
118	  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
119	  buh_e
120	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
121	  buh_line "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
122	  buh_line "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
123	  buh_line "beyond your own Claude subscription."
124	  buh_e
125	  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
126	  buh_line "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
127	  buh_e
128	
129	  # Docker gate
130	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
131	    buh_error "Docker is not available on this machine."
132	    buh_line  "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
133	    buh_e
134	    buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
135	    buh_e
136	    return 0
137	  fi
138	
139	  buh_line "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
140	  buh_line "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
141	  buh_e
142	
143	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
144	
145	  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
146	  buh_e
147	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
148	  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
149	  buh_line "identified by a timestamp tag."
150	  buh_e
151	  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
152	  buh_line "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
153	  buh_line "container without cloud build involvement.  Note this only builds for the"
154	  buh_line "host platform."
155	  buh_e
156	
157	  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY}"
158	  buh_e
159	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
160	  buh_line "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
161	  buh_line "are reachable from inside."
162	  buh_e
163	  buh_line "Build it:"
164	  buh_e
165	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" "" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
166	  buh_e
167	  buh_line "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
168	  buh_line "  ${z_example_tag}"
169	  buh_line "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
170	  buh_e
171	
172	  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
173	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
174	  else
175	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
176	  fi
177	  buh_e
178	
179	  buh_warn "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
180	  buh_line "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
181	  buh_line "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
182	  buh_e
183	
184	  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
185	  buh_e
186	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
187	  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
188	  buh_e
189	  buh_line "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
190	  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
191	  buh_line "automatically:"
192	  buh_e
193	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
194	  buh_e
195	  buh_line "Two ${RBYC_KLUDGE} commands, two different jobs:"
196	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
197	  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
198	  buh_e
199	  buh_line "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
200	  buh_line "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
201	  buh_line "so the tool does the driving for you."
202	  buh_e
203	
204	  # Bottle kludge image probe
205	  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
206	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
207	  else
208	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
209	  fi
210	  buh_e
211	
212	  # =================================================================
213	  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
214	  # =================================================================
215	  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
216	  buh_e
217	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
218	  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
219	  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
220	  buh_e
221	  buh_code "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
222	  buh_e
223	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
224	  buh_line "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
225	  buh_line "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
226	  buh_e
227	  buh_line "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
228	  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
229	  buh_e
230	  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
231	  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
232	  buh_line "now you see the mechanism underneath."
233	  buh_e
234	
235	  # Hallmark probes
236	  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
237	    buh_line " [*] ${z_code_sentry_field} = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
238	  else
239	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
240	  fi
241	  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
242	    buh_line " [*] ${z_code_bottle_field} = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
243	  else
244	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
245	  fi
246	  buh_e
247	
248	  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
249	  buh_line "clean tree:"
250	  buh_e
251	  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
252	  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
253	  buh_e
254	
255	  # =================================================================
256	  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
257	  # =================================================================
258	  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
259	  buh_e
260	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
261	  buh_e
262	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}    — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
263	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
264	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
265	  buh_e
266	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
267	  buh_line "network directly — everything routes through the ${RBYC_SENTRY_P} rules."
268	  buh_e
269	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
270	  buh_e
271	  buh_line "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
272	  buh_line "Docker, creates the ${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network, starts the containers,"
273	  buh_line "waits for the ${RBYC_SENTRY} to confirm its iptables rules are"
274	  buh_line "applied, then starts the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}."
275	  buh_e
276	
277	  # Charged probe
278	  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
279	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
280	  else
281	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
282	  fi
283	  buh_e
284	
285	  # =================================================================
286	  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
287	  # =================================================================
288	  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
289	  buh_e
290	  buh_line "SSH into the bottle:"
291	  buh_e
292	  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
293	  buh_e
294	  buh_line "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
295	  buh_line "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
296	  buh_e
297	  buh_code "   claude"
298	  buh_e
299	  buh_line "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
300	  buh_line "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
301	  buh_line "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
302	  buh_e
303	  buh_warn "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
304	  buh_line "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
305	  buh_line "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
306	  buh_line "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
307	  buh_line "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
308	  buh_e
309	  buh_line "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
310	  buh_line "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
311	  buh_line "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
312	  buh_line "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
313	  buh_e
314	  buh_line "Try your first interaction:"
315	  buh_e
316	  buh_code "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
317	  buh_e
318	  buh_line "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
319	  buh_line "The workspace is a bind mount — edits inside the bottle are"
320	  buh_line "visible on the host filesystem, and vice versa. Your repo"
321	  buh_line "working tree gets dirty when you work inside the bottle,"
322	  buh_line "and that is the point."
323	  buh_e
324	  buh_line "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
325	  buh_e
326	  buh_line "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
327	  buh_line "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
328	  buh_line "login session with full terminal negotiation."
329	  buh_e
330	  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
331	  buh_line "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
332	  buh_e
333	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
334	  buh_e
335	
336	  # =================================================================
337	  # Step 5: Verify network containment
338	  # =================================================================
339	  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
340	  buh_e
341	  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
342	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and www.internic.net (a test"
343	  buh_line "target — ICANN-owned, stable). Everything else is blocked."
344	  buh_e
345	  buh_line "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
346	  buh_e
347	  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
348	  buh_line "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
349	  buh_e
350	  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
351	  buh_line "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
352	  buh_e
353	  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://www.internic.net"
354	  buh_line "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
355	  buh_e
356	  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
357	  buh_line "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
358	  buh_e
359	  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
360	  buh_line "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
361	  buh_e
362	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} enforces this with two layers:"
363	  buh_line "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
364	  buh_line "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
365	  buh_line "must agree for traffic to pass."
366	  buh_e
367	  buh_line "www.internic.net is included in the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}"
368	  buh_line "specifically for this verification step — it proves the"
369	  buh_line "allowlist works for a non-Anthropic domain."
370	  buh_e
371	
372	  # =================================================================
373	  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
374	  # =================================================================
375	  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
376	  buh_e
377	  buh_line "${RBYC_QUENCH} stops and removes all three containers and the"
378	  buh_line "${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network:"
379	  buh_e
380	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
381	  buh_e
382	  buh_line "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
383	  buh_line "reuses them instantly."
384	  buh_e
385	
386	  # =================================================================
387	  # Iteration loop
388	  # =================================================================
389	  buh_section "The iteration loop"
390	  buh_e
391	  buh_line "You now have the full local cycle:"
392	  buh_e
393	  buh_line "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
394	  buh_line "  2. Commit your changes (${RBYC_KLUDGE} needs a clean tree)"
395	  buh_line "  3. ${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
396	  buh_tt    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
397	  buh_line  "  4. Commit the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} change"
398	  buh_line  "  5. ${RBYC_CHARGE}: Stop and restart"
399	  buh_tt    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
400	  buh_line  "  6. SSH in:"
401	  buh_line  "       ${z_cmd_ssh}"
402	  buh_line  "  7. Test your changes"
403	  buh_e
404	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
405	  buh_line "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
406	  buh_e
407	  buh_line "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The ${RBYC_SENTRY} rarely changes, so"
408	  buh_line "you almost never re-kludge it — the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your iteration"
409	  buh_line "target."
410	  buh_e
411	
412	  # --- Return to start ---
413	  buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
414	  buh_e
415	}
416	
417	# eof
418

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - First Crucible (local builds)

set -euo pipefail

test -z "${ZRBHOFC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBHOFC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# First Crucible — local builds onboarding
#
# Frame 4-refined handbook: teaching prose + probes + tabtarget refs.
# Target learner: crucible explorer — local-only, zero cloud, fast
# iteration for security exploration. Zero registry, zero SA credentials.
#
# Nameplate: ccyolo  (Claude Code sandbox, Anthropic-only network)
# Vessels:   rbev-sentry-deb-tether (sentry), rbev-bottle-ccyolo (bottle)
#
# ₢A6AAC DRAFT — content ready for implementation review.
# Infrastructure gaps are marked with [INFRA-NEEDED] comments.
# Assumptions are marked with [ASSUMPTION].

rbho_first_crucible() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"

  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-fk";                 local -r z_cmd_rbw_fk="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "rbw-cKB";                local -r z_cmd_rbw_cKB="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=";  local -r z_cmd_sentry_hallmark="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}";     local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_sentry_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_bottle_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "k260413155458-f9dcb6d9"; local -r z_example_tag="${z_buym_yelp}"

  # --- Probes ---

  # Docker runtime
  local z_has_docker=0
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1

  # Nameplate exists
  local z_nameplate_exists=0
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1

  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
  local z_sentry_hallmark=""
  local z_bottle_hallmark=""
  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0
  local z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
  fi

  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
  local z_sentry_image_exists=0
  local z_bottle_image_exists=0
  local z_line=""
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    local -r z_fc_images_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}4_repotag.txt"
    local -r z_fc_images_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
         > "${z_fc_images_out}" 2>"${z_fc_images_err}"; then
      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          *"${z_sentry_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_sentry_image_exists=1 ;;
          *"${z_bottle_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_bottle_image_exists=1 ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_fc_images_out}"
    fi
  fi

  # Crucible charged
  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
  local z_crucible_charged=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    local -r z_fc_ps_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_PS_PREFIX}2_names.txt"
    local -r z_fc_ps_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}6_ps.txt"
    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" > "${z_fc_ps_out}" 2>"${z_fc_ps_err}"; then
      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
        case "${z_line}" in "${z_moniker}-bottle") z_crucible_charged=1; break ;; esac
      done < "${z_fc_ps_out}"
    fi
  fi

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a Crucible Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_line "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_line "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_line "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_line "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_error "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_line  "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_line "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_line "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
  buh_line "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a vessel image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_line "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_line "container without cloud build involvement.  Note this only builds for the"
  buh_line "host platform."
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_line "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_line "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Build it:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_KLUDGE_VESSEL}" "" " ${z_sentry_vessel}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The output prints a hallmark tag — something like"
  buh_line "  ${z_example_tag}"
  buh_line "Copy it. You will paste it into the nameplate in Step 2."
  buh_e

  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Kludge-tagged sentry image found locally"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No kludge-tagged sentry image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_warn "Kludge requires a clean git tree."
  buh_line "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  This is by"
  buh_line "design — container images not backed by git commits easy to confuse."
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} bottle includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This command is different from the sentry kludge. It builds the"
  buh_line "bottle AND drives the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the nameplate"
  buh_line "automatically:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Two ${RBYC_KLUDGE} commands, two different jobs:"
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_fk}  — standalone vessel kludge. Produces a hallmark, touches nothing else."
  buh_line "  ${z_cmd_rbw_cKB} — nameplate-aware bottle kludge. Builds AND installs the hallmark."
  buh_e
  buh_line "The sentry is shared across nameplates, so you kludge it standalone"
  buh_line "and drive the hallmark yourself. The bottle belongs to one nameplate,"
  buh_line "so the tool does the driving for you."
  buh_e

  # Bottle kludge image probe
  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Kludge-tagged bottle image found locally"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No kludge-tagged bottle image found"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 2: Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Drive sentry hallmark into the nameplate"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a Crucible — it specifies"
  buh_line "what ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RBRN} file has two hallmark fields — one for the sentry"
  buh_line "and one for the bottle. The bottle kludge (Step 1.2) already drove"
  buh_line "its hallmark. Now you drive the sentry hallmark by hand."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Open the nameplate file and paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  buh_line "into the ${z_cmd_sentry_hallmark} field."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This is deliberately manual — it teaches you what ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} are and"
  buh_line "where they live. You just saw the automated version with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE};"
  buh_line "now you see the mechanism underneath."
  buh_e

  # Hallmark probes
  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_line " [*] ${z_code_sentry_field} = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK is empty — paste the sentry hallmark from Step 1.1"
  fi
  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_line " [*] ${z_code_bottle_field} = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK is empty — run Step 1.2 (bottle kludge)"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "After editing, commit the change — the next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} will need a"
  buh_line "clean tree:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Drive sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Charge the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your nameplate:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}    — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the bottle"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} mediates all traffic. The bottle never touches the"
  buh_line "network directly — everything routes through the ${RBYC_SENTRY_P} rules."
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Charge takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
  buh_line "Docker, creates the ${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network, starts the containers,"
  buh_line "waits for the ${RBYC_SENTRY} to confirm its iptables rules are"
  buh_line "applied, then starts the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}."
  buh_e

  # Charged probe
  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}Crucible ${z_moniker} is charged (bottle container running)"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}Crucible ${z_moniker} is not charged"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
  buh_e
  buh_line "SSH into the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
  buh_line "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   claude"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Claude Code will prompt you to authenticate. It opens a URL —"
  buh_line "copy it to your workstation browser, sign in with your Claude"
  buh_line "subscription, and paste the code back into the terminal."
  buh_e
  buh_warn "The ccyolo bottle pins Claude Code to a specific version."
  buh_line "Versions after v2.1.89 have a regression where paste does not"
  buh_line "work in the OAuth input prompt through SSH or docker exec"
  buh_line "(github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/47745). If you"
  buh_line "update the pin and paste stops working, type the code manually."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Once authenticated, Claude Code starts in full autonomy mode —"
  buh_line "no permission prompts. Inside a network-contained crucible,"
  buh_line "this is the correct posture: the sentry enforces the real"
  buh_line "security boundary, not the tool permission system."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Try your first interaction:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   The count_words.sh script has bugs — can you find and fix them?"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Watch Claude read the files, identify issues, and edit the code."
  buh_line "The workspace is a bind mount — edits inside the bottle are"
  buh_line "visible on the host filesystem, and vice versa. Your repo"
  buh_line "working tree gets dirty when you work inside the bottle,"
  buh_line "and that is the point."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Why SSH instead of docker exec?"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Docker exec is laggy and breaks terminal resize — Claude Code's"
  buh_line "interactive display needs correct dimensions. SSH gives a proper"
  buh_line "login session with full terminal negotiation."
  buh_e
  buh_line "If SSH fails, ${RBYC_RACK} is the diagnostic fallback —"
  buh_line "docker exec into the bottle to inspect state:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 5: Verify network containment
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Verify network containment"
  buh_e
  buh_line "From inside the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} (while SSH'd in), you can test what's reachable."
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} nameplate allows Anthropic and www.internic.net (a test"
  buh_line "target — ICANN-owned, stable). Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Run these curl commands inside the bottle:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://api.anthropic.com"
  buh_line "   Expected: 404 (API wants auth, not bare GET)"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://claude.ai"
  buh_line "   Expected: 403 (web app)"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://www.internic.net"
  buh_line "   Expected: 200 (test target on allowlist)"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://google.com"
  buh_line "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 5 https://registry.npmjs.org"
  buh_line "   Expected: 000 or timeout (blocked)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} enforces this with two layers:"
  buh_line "dnsmasq resolves only whitelisted domains; iptables drops"
  buh_line "packets to any IP not in the CIDR allowlist. Both layers"
  buh_line "must agree for traffic to pass."
  buh_e
  buh_line "www.internic.net is included in the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}"
  buh_line "specifically for this verification step — it proves the"
  buh_line "allowlist works for a non-Anthropic domain."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 6: Quench the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Quench the crucible"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_QUENCH} stops and removes all three containers and the"
  buh_line "${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Clean shutdown. The images stay cached locally — your next charge"
  buh_line "reuses them instantly."
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Iteration loop
  # =================================================================
  buh_section "The iteration loop"
  buh_e
  buh_line "You now have the full local cycle:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  1. Edit the Dockerfile or entrypoint"
  buh_line "  2. Commit your changes (${RBYC_KLUDGE} needs a clean tree)"
  buh_line "  3. ${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
  buh_tt    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_line  "  4. Commit the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} change"
  buh_line  "  5. ${RBYC_CHARGE}: Stop and restart"
  buh_tt    "       " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_line  "  6. SSH in:"
  buh_line  "       ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_line  "  7. Test your changes"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} tears down any prior state before starting, so you"
  buh_line "don't need to Quench between iterations — just Charge again."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Steps 3-7 take under a minute. The ${RBYC_SENTRY} rarely changes, so"
  buh_line "you almost never re-kludge it — the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your iteration"
  buh_line "target."
  buh_e

  # --- Return to start ---
  buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buh_e
}

# eof

file.content:
#   via direct bash variable interpolation:
#     buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
#   Yelps are pre-rendered (literal ESC bytes) and must not contain %.

buh_line() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_RESET}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

######################################################################
# Public: Semantic line functions
#
# Each routes through buyf_format_yawp with a BUYC_* ambient color,
# then prints via indent-aware printf.  Diastema markers in the string
# are resolved to ANSI/OSC-8 at display time.
#
# buh_line   — prose default (BUYC_RESET ambient)
# buh_code   — command/code (BUYC_CYAN ambient)
# buh_warn   — warning (BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW ambient)
# buh_error  — error (BUYC_BRIGHT_RED ambient)

buh_code() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_CYAN}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

buh_warn() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_YELLOW}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

buh_error() {
  zbuh_sentinel
  buyf_format_yawp "${BUYC_BRIGHT_RED}" "${1:-}"
  printf '%b\n' "${z_buh_body_indent}${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

content.text:
Good — all of `buh_section`, `buh_step1`, `buh_step2`, `buh_line`, `buh_code`, `buh_warn` go through `buyf_format_yawp`, which resolves diastemas. So yelps work in all of them.

Now let me audit every bare word systematically. I need a few new variant constants first — let me check which plurals/variants I'll need.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Vocabulary — linked term constants
#
# Kindled after regime loads (needs RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL).  Does not
# source or kindle anything itself — the consuming _cli.sh is responsible
# for sourcing buym_yelp.sh and this file, then calling zrbyc_kindle.
#
# Each constant is a diastema-marked yelp fragment suitable for
# interpolation into buh_line calls:
#   buh_line "A ${RBYC_DEPOT} is where images live."
#
# Variant forms (_S plural, _P possessive) link to the base anchor
# with alternate display text.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBYC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBYC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Module kindle

zrbyc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbyc already kindled"

  zbuym_sentinel

  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"

  # Helper: yawp then capture into named readonly
  # Usage: zrbyc_yk RBYC_TERM base_url anchor [display]
  zrbyc_yk() {
    buyy_link_yawp "${2}" "${3}" "${4:-}"
    readonly "${1}=${z_buym_yelp}"
  }

  # --- Paddock anchor inventory (38 base terms) ---

  # Operations
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ORDAIN    "${z_docs}" "Ordain"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CONJURE   "${z_docs}" "Conjure"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BIND      "${z_docs}" "Bind"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_GRAFT     "${z_docs}" "Graft"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_KLUDGE    "${z_docs}" "Kludge"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ENSHRINE  "${z_docs}" "Enshrine"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SUMMON    "${z_docs}" "Summon"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PLUMB     "${z_docs}" "Plumb"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TALLY     "${z_docs}" "Tally"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VOUCH     "${z_docs}" "Vouch"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LEVY      "${z_docs}" "Levy"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CHARGE    "${z_docs}" "Charge"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_QUENCH    "${z_docs}" "Quench"

  # Artifacts and infrastructure
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VESSEL    "${z_docs}" "Vessel"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_HALLMARK  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DEPOT     "${z_docs}" "Depot"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RELIQUARY "${z_docs}" "Reliquary"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_POUCH     "${z_docs}" "Pouch"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_NAMEPLATE "${z_docs}" "Nameplate"

  # Egress modes
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TETHERED  "${z_docs}" "Tethered"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_AIRGAP    "${z_docs}" "Airgap"

  # Crucible components
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CRUCIBLE  "${z_docs}" "Crucible"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ENCLAVE   "${z_docs}" "Enclave"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY    "${z_docs}" "Sentry"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY_S  "${z_docs}" "Sentry" "Sentries"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_SENTRY_P  "${z_docs}" "Sentry" "Sentry's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PENTACLE  "${z_docs}" "Pentacle"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BOTTLE    "${z_docs}" "Bottle"

  # Roles
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PAYOR     "${z_docs}" "Payor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_GOVERNOR  "${z_docs}" "Governor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DIRECTOR  "${z_docs}" "Director"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RETRIEVER "${z_docs}" "Retriever"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CHARTER   "${z_docs}" "Charter"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_KNIGHT    "${z_docs}" "Knight"

  # Infrastructure concepts
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TABTARGET "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REGIME    "${z_docs}" "Regime"

  # Regime file types
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRP      "${z_docs}" "RBRP"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRR      "${z_docs}" "RBRR"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRN      "${z_docs}" "RBRN"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRV      "${z_docs}" "RBRV"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BURC      "${z_docs}" "BURC"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_BURS      "${z_docs}" "BURS"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRO      "${z_docs}" "RBRO"

  # --- Additional anchors used in handbooks ---

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE "${z_docs}" "" "Recipe Bottle"

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RACK      "${z_docs}" "Rack"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_MANOR     "${z_docs}" "Manor"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RBRA      "${z_docs}" "RBRA"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_CCYOLO    "${z_docs}" "ccyolo"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_ABJURE    "${z_docs}" "Abjure"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REKON     "${z_docs}" "Rekon"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LOG       "${z_docs}" "Log"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TRANSCRIPT "${z_docs}" "Transcript"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_OUTPUT    "${z_docs}" "Output"

  # --- Variant forms (plural, possessive) ---

  zrbyc_yk RBYC_HALLMARKS  "${z_docs}" "Hallmark"   "Hallmarks"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VESSELS    "${z_docs}" "Vessel"     "Vessels"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_DIRECTORS  "${z_docs}" "Director"   "Directors"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_RETRIEVERS "${z_docs}" "Retriever"  "Retrievers"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_TABTARGETS "${z_docs}" "Tabtarget"  "Tabtargets"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_REGIMES    "${z_docs}" "Regime"     "Regimes"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_LOGS       "${z_docs}" "Log"        "Logs"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_MANORS     "${z_docs}" "Manor"      "Manor's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_PAYORS     "${z_docs}" "Payor"      "Payor's"
  zrbyc_yk RBYC_VOUCHED    "${z_docs}" "Vouch"      "Vouched"

  # --- Probe markers (pass/warn for handbook status probes) ---

  buyy_pass_yawp " [*] "; readonly RBYC_PROBE_YES="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_warn_yawp " [ ] "; readonly RBYC_PROBE_NO="${z_buym_yelp}"

  unset -f zrbyc_yk
  readonly ZRBYC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbyc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBYC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbyc not kindled - call zrbyc_kindle first"
}

# eof

Do you see the issue in the image?  That yelp stitching failed.  Do you see why?[Image #1]
[Image: source: /Users/bhyslop/.claude/image-cache/2d0d7318-15e0-4358-80e2-49453a4971ec/1.png]

Yes — the underline from `RBYC_CCYOLO` bleeds through the space and into `RBYC_BOTTLE`, and then continues through the rest of the line. The `buh_warn` ambient color is bright yellow, but the link yelp (`BUYC_LINK = \033[97;4m`) sets underline (`;4m`). After the link text ends, the resolver restores the ambient color — but the ambient doesn't explicitly clear underline. So the underline attribute persists.

Let me check how the link resolver resets.
